notes: Excellent execution overall. Metrics are accurate: DC ratio 5.69, NJ 1.07, national 0.55, top 5 concentration 37.98%, top 10 at 61.32%, district hotspots correctly identified. Three-level analysis (state ratios, portfolio concentration, district granularity) is complete and well-structured. Risk tiers properly defined and categorized; DC flagged as outlier; <100 payee threshold acknowledged. Recommendations are specific and tied to findings (stress-test NJ/CA/NY, investigate DC, monitor Midwest cluster). Minor gaps: (1) The distinction between high-ratio/low-dollar risk (e.g., ND at 0.99 ratio but only $596K) vs. high-ratio/high-dollar risk (NJ, CA, NY) could be more prominent and explicit earlier in report. (2) Nuance that ratio alone is not sufficient predictor of total-system risk is mentioned but could be emphasized more clearly. Work demonstrates strong analytical rigor and domain understanding; these are refinements to already-solid output.
